headlines.text: US awaits Iran's response to war-ending peace proposal as Strait of Hormuz clashes continue
Trump announces 3-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire for Victory Day, 1,000 prisoners to be swapped
Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship MV Hondius arrives at Tenerife; 3 dead, 100+ still aboard
S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit new all-time highs on strong April jobs report; Dow barely budges
National gas average hits $4.53, up $1.40 from a year ago, as Iran war squeezes supply
Virginia Supreme Court blocks Democratic redistricting referendum, major blow to party's midterm hopes
Hezbollah claims 9 attacks on Israel Saturday; IDF orders evacuations in southern Lebanon
Russia holds downsized Victory Day parade; Putin invokes WWII to rally troops in Ukraine
Devil Wears Prada 2 surpasses original film's $326M global lifetime gross in just 9 days
Blue Dot Fever: Post Malone, Meghan Trainor, Zayn, and Pussycat Dolls all cancel tours over unsold seats

breaking.text: BREAKING: US awaits formal Iranian response to peace proposal; sporadic Hormuz clashes reported Saturday morning

stories.hed: Iran Still Ghosting Washington on Peace Deal as Hormuz Clashes Simmer
stories.dek: The US is waiting on Iran's response to a 14-point war-ending proposal — Rubio says their system is 'highly fractured and dysfunctional.' Meanwhile the Navy keeps playing bumper boats with Iranian tankers, and Trump calls it a 'love tap.' Ceasefire, allegedly, still in effect.

stories.hed: Russia's Victory Day Parade Goes On, Scaled Back and Surrounded by Drones That Didn't Show
stories.dek: Putin held his annual Red Square parade — this year with extra security and North Korean troops in attendance, because 2026. Zelenskyy issued a cheeky decree 'permitting' Russia to celebrate, which the Kremlin called a 'silly joke.'

stories.hed: Virginia Supreme Court Kills Democratic Redistricting Win, Handing GOP a Lifeline
stories.dek: The Virginia Supreme Court blocked a referendum passed in April that would have given Democrats up to four additional US House seats. Republicans, who had been staring down a brutal midterm map, exhaled collectively.

stories.hed: Hantavirus Cruise Ship Crisis: MV Hondius Docks at Tenerife With 3 Dead, WHO Chief On Scene
stories.dek: A hantavirus outbreak aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship has killed three people, with more than 100 passengers still aboard as the ship reaches the Canary Islands. Spanish authorities in biohazard suits are waiting at port, as is the WHO director, who apparently had nothing better to do this Saturday.

stories.hed: Blue Dot Fever Is the Music Industry's Collective Reckoning With Its Own Greed
stories.dek: Post Malone, Meghan Trainor, Zayn, Jelly Roll, and the Pussycat Dolls have all canceled or gutted their 2026 tours. The blue dots on Ticketmaster maps — indicating unsold seats — are everywhere, and insiders say the industry has been 'high on its own supply' for years. Average ticket price in 2026: $144. Average fan's patience: gone.

markets.context: S&P and Nasdaq hit fresh all-time highs Friday as a blowout April jobs report (115K added vs. 65K expected) let investors briefly forget there is a war in the Middle East.

gas.note: Filling up your tank now costs roughly what a nice dinner used to — thanks, Strait of Hormuz.

popCulture.hed: Devil Wears Prada 2 Blows Past Original Film's Entire Lifetime Gross in Under 10 Days
popCulture.dek: The Meryl Streep and Anne Hathaway sequel hit $324M worldwide through Thursday, surpassing the original's $326M lifetime haul with the weekend still ahead. Miranda Priestly remains the most powerful force in Western civilization, apparently.

genZ.definition: A term coined this week to describe the embarrassing phenomenon of major music artists canceling tours because their Ticketmaster seating maps are full of blue dots — the color used to show unsold seats. Post Malone, Meghan Trainor, Zayn Malik, and the Pussycat Dolls have all been diagnosed in the past week, with insiders saying artists have been pricing tickets like they are Taylor Swift when they are decidedly not.
genZ.usage: Iran still has not responded to the US peace deal — honestly sounds like a bad case of Blue Dot Fever, lots of open seats at the negotiating table.

tldr: The US is holding its breath waiting for Iran to respond to a peace proposal that would end a 71-day war, while simultaneously firing on tankers and calling it a ceasefire. Elsewhere, stocks hit record highs, gas is $4.53 a gallon, and Miranda Priestly made $324 million in nine days — so the economy is, depending on your portfolio, fine.
